ea_prompt.parent_head_capture_note: Captured at SDO authoring time. EA must re-capture at the start of execution via `git rev-parse HEAD` on BOTH repos and substitute live values into the comprehension comment. If EA-1 lands on devplatform main or BlarAI main between SDO authoring and EA-2 dispatch (parallel window permits this), EA-2 proceeds against the live head; working sets are disjoint, so rebase is not expected. Note the delta in the comprehension comment for the auditor.

attachment.reason: Authoritative scope; SS5.1 EA-2 deliverable specification; SS5.3 procedure-home + helper-script-boundary pre-decisions; SS7 parallel-with-EA-1 sequencing

Sprint 11 continuation; lessons L-12 (comprehension recitation), L-13 (parent_head capture), L-15 (working-set declaration), L-19 (cross-repo ordering), L-22 (mature-not-minimal floors), L-25 (live-computation-not-prior-text discipline)
Current state of the devplatform-side amendment surface; surgical insertion at Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 transition + Sprint Close hooks. Read end-to-end before editing.

Data TARGET (not edited in this EA). The procedure documents how to refresh CLAUDE.md SSActive State; do NOT perform a sample edit. The current SSActive State block is the reference for section ordering and phrasing conventions only.
Input data source for step (d) of the procedure -- live roster read.
Q1-1 per-file ledger schema for the EA-2 ledger entry. Sprint 11 EA-1 DEC-17 formalizes this; EA-2's ledger entry must conform.
Sprint 8 SWAGR gap #5 -- Active State drift first surfacing. Confirm exact filename via Glob at EA start.
Sprint 9 SWAGR gap #4 -- Active State drift recurrence.
Sprint 10 SWAGR SS15.3 -- Active State drift recurrence (third sprint); recommendation to codify deterministic refresh.

ea_prompt.pre_flight:
Sprint 11 EA-2 is the FIRST BlarAI sprint authoring a deterministic Active State refresh procedure. The procedure flips the polarity of every prior SSActive State edit from copy-prior-text to live-computation- first. Three sprints (8/9/10) surfaced the recurring drift shape; SDV SS3 cites this as the structural cause.
Working set verification. Before any writes, Test-Path each of: - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\ (directory; create if absent) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\ (must exist) - C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\co_lead_architect.md (must exist; surgical amendment surface) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md (must exist; READ-ONLY in this EA)
Conflict check: ensure no prior runbook of this name exists: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; Test-Path docs\runbooks\active_state_refresh.md Expected: False. If True, STOP and escalate -- do NOT overwrite.
Fleet-Pause SOP. PAUSE the fleet before any checkout or git work:
$env:PYTHONPATH = "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"; python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.pause_fleet('Sprint 11 EA-2 Active State refresh procedure', updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): pause fleet -- Sprint 11 EA-2 Active State refresh"
Resume LAST, before exit: $env:PYTHONPATH = "C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform"; python -c "from tools.autonomy_budget import state; state.resume_fleet(updated_by='ea_code', path='C:/Users/mrbla/devplatform/tools/autonomy_budget/state.json')" cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI; git commit --allow-empty -m "chore(ops): unpause fleet -- Sprint 11 EA-2 done"
API-NAME GOTCHA: function is `resume_fleet`, NOT `unpause_fleet`.
Parallel execution context (LA SDV v2). EA-1 may be running concurrently against disjoint working sets (devplatform docs/decisions/ + BlarAI docs/ledger/). DO NOT touch any EA-1 working-set path (see negative_constraints). If EA-1's devplatform commit or BlarAI ledger branch landed between SDO authoring and EA-2 dispatch, that is expected and not a conflict (disjoint paths).
ea_prompt.comprehension_gate:
BEFORE any edits, checkouts, or branch creation, post a comprehension comment to Vikunja tracking task #410 prefixed `[agent:ea_code][phase:comprehension]` containing ALL of:
1. Milestone objective in your own words: what procedure you are authoring on BlarAI; what amendment you are making to the devplatform Co-Lead wake template; why now (3-sprint Active State drift recurrence per Sprint 8 gap #5 + Sprint 9 gap #4 + Sprint 10 SS15.3); who consumes the procedure downstream (Co-Lead at Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 + Sprint Close SCR authoring).
2. Per-deliverable work-item summary. WI-1 through WI-7 -- one sentence each in your own words.
3. Files to create/modify (absolute paths). EXACTLY four files (procedure + ledger + wake-template amendment + ledger optional helper). The optional helper script counts as one additional file IF EA-2 chooses to ship it (recommended per mature-not-minimal): - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\active_state_refresh.md (new) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\active_state_refresh.ps1 (new; OPTIONAL but recommended) - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea2_active-state-refresh.md (new; TS = UTC YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS captured at EA start) - C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\co_lead_architect.md (amend; surgical insertion)
The C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md SSActive State section is NOT edited in this EA. The procedure DOCUMENTS how to edit; it does not PERFORM a sample edit.
4. Source files to read (read-only). All required_attachments above.
5. EXACT deliverable structure -- recite VERBATIM.
Procedure file (docs/runbooks/active_state_refresh.md) sections: SS1 Purpose -> SS2 Inputs -> SS3 Live-computation steps in order ((a) live pytest, (b) live git log, (c) live Vikunja MCP, (d) live active_tasks.yaml, (e) writeback to CLAUDE.md SSActive State only) -> SS4 Worked example with verbatim outputs -> SS5 Resulting Active State block -> SS6 Failure modes + remediation. Length floor: >= 50 lines.
Helper script (tools/active_state_refresh.ps1) IF shipped: PowerShell script automating SS3 steps (a)-(d) and printing the prospective SSActive State block to stdout. Does NOT write to CLAUDE.md (writeback remains Co-Lead's hand-edit). Header comment block declaring purpose, inputs, outputs; main() invokes the four data-gathering steps; prints assembled block. PowerShell preferred per SDV SS5.3 scope-simplicity rationale.
Wake-template amendment (devplatform/.../co_lead_architect.md): Surgical insertion of a new sub-section or hook block referencing the BlarAI procedure absolute path. The insertion appears at BOTH (i) the Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 transition location, and (ii) the Sprint Close (SCR-authoring) location. Each insertion >= 5 lines; aggregate >= 10 lines of new hook content. Existing template content preserved otherwise.
Ledger entry (docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea2_active-state-refresh.md): Q1-1 schema per docs/ledger/README.md. Frontmatter (ledger_id, date, sprint_id=11, entry_type=EA, predecessor, branch= feature/p5-task11-ea2-active-state-refresh, merge_commit=null, disposition=COMPLETE) -> SSSummary -> SSDeliverables -> SSFiles Changed (BlarAI commit + devplatform commit) -> SSQuality Gate -> SSDEC References (link to DEC-15 sprint-lifecycle, SS5.1 EA-2 scope; reference forthcoming DEC-17 ledger-format if EA-1 has landed).
6. ORACLE expectation (verbatim, executed at EA close after both commits land):
# BlarAI side cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git diff main...feature/p5-task11-ea2-active-state-refresh --name-only Expected (sorted, helper-shipping case): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea2_active-state-refresh.md docs/runbooks/active_state_refresh.md tools/active_state_refresh.ps1
(If helper omitted: 2 files only -- ledger + runbook.)
# devplatform side cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform git diff PRE_HEAD...HEAD --name-only Expected: docs/scheduled/wake_templates/co_lead_architect.md
Any other path on either side -> ORACLE violation; STOP and report.
7. Working-set declaration (L-15). Verbatim: "I write to exactly four absolute paths (three if I omit the optional helper): BlarAI procedure runbook + BlarAI ledger entry + (optional) BlarAI helper script + devplatform Co-Lead wake-template amendment. I read CLAUDE.md SSActive State for phrasing reference but do NOT edit it in this EA. I read active_tasks.yaml as a data source. I touch no other file in either repo. If I discover a defect in any source file or a contradiction between governance text and operational reality, I STOP and escalate via the comprehension comment; I do NOT 'reconcile' by unilaterally editing either side."
8. Mature-not-minimal acknowledgment (L-22). Verbatim: "Mature not minimal: procedure >= 50 lines; wake-template hook insertions aggregate >= 10 lines; helper script (if shipped) is a coherent runnable artifact, not a placeholder. The worked example in SS4 of the procedure is an ACTUAL run from the Sprint 11 kickoff state Co-Lead can verify reproduces -- not a templated stub. Padding to hit the floor is rejected."
9. Live-computation-not-prior-text discipline (L-25). Verbatim: "Every Active State refresh STARTS with live computation, NOT with prior text. Prior text is a REFERENCE for section ordering and phrasing conventions, NOT a data source. The procedure encodes this polarity inversion as its core rule; the worked example demonstrates it concretely; the wake-template hook reminds Co-Lead of it at every invocation."
10. Cross-repo ordering acknowledgment (L-19). Verbatim: "EA-2 commits to BlarAI main FIRST (via Co-Lead trusted_scope merge of feature/p5-task11-ea2-active-state-refresh); then commits to devplatform main SECOND (direct-to-main per Stage 6.7.5). BlarAI commit body cites the future devplatform companion via SCR pointer (procedure-references-itself-after-amendment cycle is acceptable; SCR will record both commit hashes); devplatform commit body cites the BlarAI merge commit hash. If devplatform dispatches before BlarAI merges due to fleet-cadence anomaly, devplatform STOPs and waits for BlarAI to land."
11. No-freelance-cross-repo-refactor acknowledgment (L-19). Verbatim: "Sprint 11 is documentation + investigation + (one optional new BlarAI helper script in this EA). DO NOT refactor any devplatform fleet code in this EA. DO NOT touch tools/autonomy_budget/ on either repo. DO NOT amend the Sprint Auditor wake template (that's EA-5's purview)."
12. CLAUDE.md non-edit acknowledgment (Sprint-11-specific). Verbatim: "I do NOT edit C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md SSActive State in this EA. The procedure documents the edit; it does not perform a sample edit. The Co-Lead invokes the procedure at Sprint 11 SCR cadence -- that invocation produces the first live data point. If I write a sample SSActive State block as part of the worked example in SS5 of the procedure, that block lives INSIDE the runbook (as illustration), NOT in CLAUDE.md."
13. Risks and ambiguities. State any unresolved understanding before starting -- especially: (a) helper-script ship-or-defer choice (recommended: ship; document rationale either way); (b) Co-Lead wake-template insertion location (Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 + Sprint Close -- verify both locations exist in current template before amendment; if only one exists, amend that one and document the asymmetry in the comprehension comment).
After posting the comprehension comment, apply label `Gate:Pending-SDO` (id 9) on Vikunja task #410 and STOP. WAIT for SDO Phase 1a review verdict before doing any work. Do NOT bundle downstream work with the comprehension comment.

milestone.objective: Author a deterministic Active State refresh procedure on BlarAI (runbook + optional helper script) describing live-computation steps that replace the recurring copy-prior-text drift pattern surfaced across Sprint 8 / 9 / 10 SWAGRs. Amend the devplatform Co-Lead Architect wake template to reference the procedure at Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 transition and Sprint Close (SCR-authoring) cadences. Land one BlarAI feature-branch commit (merged via Co-Lead trusted_scope) and one devplatform direct-to-main commit; commit bodies cross-reference. Sprint 11 SCR is the first procedural invocation.
milestone.branch: feature/p5-task11-ea2-active-state-refresh (BlarAI side); devplatform direct-to-main for wake-template amendment
milestone.size_rationale: Procedure file with worked example (~60-80 lines) + optional helper script (~40-60 lines) + wake-template amendment (~10-20 lines net) + ledger entry (~40-60 lines); aggregate ~150-220 lines across two repos.

work_items.wi:
WI-1: Pre-flight conflict + dependency check. Verify reference paths via Test-Path: - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\README.md -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\active_tasks.yaml -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\co_lead_architect.md -- must return True - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\active_state_refresh.md -- must return False (creating new) All four input paths MUST return True; the output runbook path MUST return False. Capture verbatim output in the ledger entry's SSQuality Gate section.
Inspect the devplatform Co-Lead wake template for the Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 and Sprint Close hook locations: Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\co_lead_architect.md -Pattern "Phase 3|Sprint Close|Sprint Kickoff" Record the line numbers / section headers for the insertion targets; if either location is absent, document and surface in the comprehension comment.

WI-2: Author the Active State refresh procedure. New file: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\active_state_refresh.md
Required sections (recite verbatim in comprehension): SS1 Purpose (1-2 paragraphs): cite the 3-sprint drift recurrence (Sprint 8 SWAGR gap #5 + Sprint 9 SWAGR gap #4 + Sprint 10 SWAGR SS15.3). State the polarity inversion: live-computation- first, prior-text-as-reference-only.
SS2 Inputs (bulleted list, absolute paths): - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI repo (pytest + git log) - Vikunja MCP server localhost:3456 - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\active_tasks.yaml
SS3 Live-computation steps in order, each with verbatim command: (a) pytest pass/skip count: .venv\Scripts\pytest shared/ services/ launcher/ --tb=no -q | tail (extract " passed, skipped" line) (b) BlarAI main HEAD: git log --oneline -1 main (c) Vikunja sprint state via MCP: mcp__vikunja__get_task() mcp__vikunja__list_tasks(project_id=3, filter_by="done", filter_value="false") (extract active sprint id + tracking task title + label set) (d) active_tasks.yaml read: Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\active_tasks.yaml (extract sprint_id + task_id + continuation_xml fields) (e) Writeback target: CLAUDE.md SSActive State section ONLY. NOT ACTIVE_SPRINT.md (Co-Lead-only via Sprint Kickoff Phase 3). NOT active_tasks.yaml (Co-Lead-only via roster transition).
SS4 Worked example (mature-not-minimal: actual reproducible run from Sprint 11 kickoff state, not templated placeholder): Show verbatim output of each command (a)-(d) using a fixed point-in-time snapshot the runbook reader can re-execute and obtain the same shape of output (numbers will drift; structure will not).
SS5 Resulting Active State block (illustration only, lives INSIDE the runbook, NOT in CLAUDE.md). Show the prospective text block assembled from the worked example outputs, formatted exactly as the current CLAUDE.md SSActive State section is formatted.
SS6 Failure modes + remediation (bulleted): (i) pytest collection error -> STOP; do not refresh from stale numbers; (ii) Vikunja MCP server down -> STOP; route via webUI fallback; (iii) active_tasks.yaml schema unexpected -> STOP and escalate; (iv) HEAD on a non-main branch -> STOP; refresh only against main.
Length floor: >= 50 lines. Mature-not-minimal: SS4 is an actual example with substituted values, not "" placeholders.

WI-3 (OPTIONAL but RECOMMENDED): Author the helper script. New file: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\active_state_refresh.ps1
PowerShell script automating SS3 steps (a)-(d) and printing the prospective SSActive State block to stdout. Required structure: - Header comment block: purpose, inputs, outputs, usage example. - Param() block: optional -SprintTaskId int (default: read from active_tasks.yaml). - Function Get-PytestBaseline (executes step (a), parses " passed, skipped" line). - Function Get-MainHead (executes step (b)). - Function Get-VikunjaSprintState (invokes MCP tools via the existing tools/vikunja_mcp CLI wrapper; emits sprint_id + title + label set). - Function Get-ActiveRoster (reads active_tasks.yaml). - Function Format-ActiveStateBlock (assembles the prospective block from gathered values). - Main: invoke functions in order; print assembled block; exit 0 on success, non-zero on any input failure (fail-closed).
Does NOT write to CLAUDE.md -- writeback remains Co-Lead's hand-edit. Script is invoked-on-demand at Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 + Sprint Close.
If EA-2 elects to omit the helper (acceptable per SDV SS5.3 helper- script boundary), the runbook procedure stands alone; document the omit-rationale in the ledger entry SSSummary.

WI-4: Amend the devplatform Co-Lead Architect wake template. File: C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\co_lead_architect.md
Surgical insertion at TWO locations (per WI-1 reconnaissance): (i) Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 transition section: insert a new sub-block (>= 5 lines) instructing the Co-Lead to invoke the BlarAI Active State refresh procedure at the kickoff Phase 3 cadence. Reference the procedure absolute path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\active_state_refresh.md. If a helper script is shipped (WI-3), also cite C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\active_state_refresh.ps1.
(ii) Sprint Close (SCR-authoring) section: insert a new sub-block (>= 5 lines) instructing the Co-Lead to invoke the BlarAI Active State refresh procedure as the first step of SCR SSActive State subsection authorship. Cite the same absolute path(s).
Aggregate hook content: >= 10 lines net (sum of both insertions). Existing template content preserved otherwise. The amendment is a procedural reference, NOT an automation guarantee -- Co-Lead remains the writer.
If WI-1 reconnaissance found only ONE of the two locations, amend that one and document the asymmetry in the commit body + ledger entry. STOP and escalate if NEITHER location exists.

WI-5: Commit BlarAI side. Create feature branch and commit: cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git checkout -b feature/p5-task11-ea2-active-state-refresh git add docs/runbooks/active_state_refresh.md git add tools/active_state_refresh.ps1 (IF shipped) git add docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea2_active-state-refresh.md git commit -m "[sprint:11][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-2 Active State refresh procedure + (optional) helper + ledger"
Commit body cites: (a) the SWAGR-chain motivation (Sprint 8 gap #5, Sprint 9 gap #4, Sprint 10 SS15.3); (b) the devplatform companion commit via SCR pointer (will be recorded at sprint close; do NOT attempt to embed the future hash); (c) the procedure's polarity- inversion rule (live-computation-first) in one sentence.
DO NOT merge to BlarAI main yourself. Leave for Co-Lead trusted_scope merge at Phase 3.

WI-6: BlarAI ledger entry on the feature branch. Path: C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea2_active-state-refresh.md where TS = UTC YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS captured at EA start.
Frontmatter (Q1-1 schema per docs/ledger/README.md): --- ledger_id: TS_sprint11_ea2_active-state-refresh date: YYYY-MM-DD sprint_id: 11 entry_type: EA predecessor: branch: feature/p5-task11-ea2-active-state-refresh merge_commit: null disposition: COMPLETE ---
Body sections: SSSummary (3-5 sentences): polarity inversion rationale; helper ship-or-omit choice + rationale; cross-repo ordering note. SSDeliverables (bulleted): procedure file path + line count; (optional) helper path + line count; wake-template amendment path + net-line-count; this ledger entry. SSFiles Changed (two sub-tables): BlarAI commit hash + paths; devplatform commit hash + path (recorded after WI-7 lands). SSQuality Gate (verbatim WI-1 Test-Path outputs + WI-1 wake- template reconnaissance line numbers). SSDEC References: link to DEC-15 sprint-lifecycle (procedure consumed at kickoff Phase 3 + SCR cadences); if EA-1 has landed DEC-17 by EA-2 completion, reference the Q1-1 ledger-format DEC; otherwise note "DEC-17 pending Sprint 11 EA-1 close".
Length floor: >= 40 lines content (excluding frontmatter).

WI-7: Commit devplatform side direct-to-main. After WI-5 BlarAI commit hash captured: cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform git add docs/scheduled/wake_templates/co_lead_architect.md git commit -m "[sprint:11][role:ea_code][phase:completion] EA-2 Co-Lead wake-template hook -- Active State refresh procedure reference"
Commit body cites: (a) the BlarAI merge commit hash (captured after Co-Lead trusted_scope merge of WI-5 -- if not yet merged at WI-7 time, cite the BlarAI feature-branch commit hash with a note that the merge commit follows); (b) the BlarAI procedure absolute path; (c) one-sentence summary of the hook insertion content.
Direct-to-main per Stage 6.7.5 devplatform convention; no feature branch on devplatform.

ea_prompt.negative_constraints:
DO NOT, under any circumstances:
1. Edit C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\CLAUDE.md SSActive State (or any other section) in this EA. The procedure DOCUMENTS the edit; it does NOT perform a sample edit. The Co-Lead's Sprint 11 SCR invocation produces the first live data point.
2. Edit C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\ACTIVE_SPRINT.md or C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\active_tasks.yaml. Those files are Co-Lead-owned via Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 / roster-transition conventions; the procedure READS active_tasks.yaml as a data source but does not write it.
3. Refactor tools/autonomy_budget/ on either repo. The procedure READS pytest output, git log, Vikunja MCP, and YAML -- existing interfaces only.
4. Touch any EA-1 working-set path (per LA parallel authorization): - C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\decisions\DEC-16_*.md - C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\decisions\DEC-17_*.md - C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\decisions\DEC-18_*.md - C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\ledger\TS_sprint11_ea1_dec-bundle.md (EA-1's own ledger entry; EA-2's ledger entry is a SEPARATE file with a DIFFERENT timestamp)
5. Touch any future-EA path. No edits to docs/sprints/_templates/ (EA-3), docs/sprints/sprint_11/test_baseline_drift_investigation.md (EA-4), .github/copilot-instructions.md, tools/vikunja_mcp/README.md, docs/scheduled/wake_templates/sprint_auditor.md (EA-5).
6. Touch any production source code or test file. No edits to services/, shared/, launcher/, tests/, pyproject.toml, conftest.py on either repo.
7. Amend ADRs or any existing DEC. EA-2 produces a procedure runbook and a wake-template amendment -- not DECs.
8. Amend any other wake template. Only co_lead_architect.md is in scope; sprint_auditor.md / ea_code.md / sdo.md remain untouched.
9. Push to a remote main without going through Co-Lead trusted_scope merge for BlarAI side. Devplatform wake-template commit is direct-to-main per Stage 6.7.5; Co-Lead reviews via SCR.
10. Bundle downstream work with the comprehension comment. Post comprehension; apply Gate:Pending-SDO; STOP. Wait for SDO Phase 1a verdict.
ea_prompt.mature_not_minimal: Procedure file >= 50 lines with SUBSTANTIVE worked example in SS4 (actual reproducible run from Sprint 11 kickoff state, not templated placeholder). Helper script (if shipped) >= 40 lines of coherent PowerShell, each function with a doc-comment, fail-closed exit code handling. Wake-template hook insertions aggregate >= 10 lines net across both Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 and Sprint Close locations. Ledger entry >= 40 lines content (excluding frontmatter), with SSFiles Changed table populated for BOTH commits. Padding to hit floors is rejected; a focused 48-line procedure with substantive content beats a 75-line procedure padded with redundant boilerplate.
ea_prompt.cross_repo_ordering: EA-2 commits to BlarAI main FIRST (feature branch feature/p5-task11-ea2-active-state-refresh, merged via Co-Lead trusted_scope at Phase 3). THEN commits to devplatform main SECOND (direct-to-main per Stage 6.7.5), with the commit body citing the BlarAI merge commit hash (or feature-branch commit hash if merge has not yet landed at WI-7 time, with a note that the merge follows). The BlarAI commit body cites the future devplatform companion via SCR pointer (the SCR records both hashes at sprint close). If devplatform dispatches before BlarAI merges due to fleet-cadence anomaly, devplatform STOPs and waits for BlarAI to land.
ea_prompt.oracle:
At EA close, after both commits land:
# BlarAI side cd C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI git diff main...feature/p5-task11-ea2-active-state-refresh --name-only Expected (sorted, helper-shipping case -- 3 files): docs/ledger/TS_sprint11_ea2_active-state-refresh.md docs/runbooks/active_state_refresh.md tools/active_state_refresh.ps1 (If helper omitted: 2 files only -- ledger + runbook.)
# devplatform side cd C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform git diff PRE_HEAD...HEAD --name-only Expected (1 file): docs/scheduled/wake_templates/co_lead_architect.md
Any other path on either side = ORACLE violation. EA captures both diffs verbatim in the completion comment on Vikunja task #410.
Additional verification commands for Co-Lead audit: (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\docs\runbooks\active_state_refresh.md | Measure-Object -Line).Lines Test-Path C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\active_state_refresh.ps1 (Get-Content C:\Users\mrbla\BlarAI\tools\active_state_refresh.ps1 | Measure-Object -Line).Lines # if shipped Select-String -Path C:\Users\mrbla\devplatform\docs\scheduled\wake_templates\co_lead_architect.md -Pattern "active_state_refresh" # Expected: >= 2 matches (Sprint Kickoff Phase 3 + Sprint Close insertions)
ea_prompt.completion:
After ORACLE passes, post `[agent:ea_code][phase:completion]` comment on Vikunja tracking task #410 containing: 1. Both commit hashes (BlarAI feature-branch commit + devplatform direct-to-main commit). If BlarAI feature branch has been merged by Co-Lead trusted_scope before EA-2 posts completion, include the merge commit hash too. 2. Both git diff --name-only outputs verbatim. 3. Line counts: procedure file, helper script (if shipped), wake- template net new-line count, ledger entry. 4. The TS timestamp used in the ledger filename. 5. Helper ship-or-omit choice + one-sentence rationale. 6. Confirmation that all WI-1 Test-Path checks returned the expected shape (3 inputs True, 1 output False). 7. Confirmation that no negative constraint was violated -- specifically: CLAUDE.md was NOT edited; ACTIVE_SPRINT.md was NOT edited; active_tasks.yaml was NOT edited.
Apply label Gate:Pending-SDO (id 9) on task #410. STOP. WAIT for SDO Phase 1b completion-review verdict.
Resume the fleet LAST BEFORE posting the completion comment -- ensures the fleet is unpaused for SDO's review-wake to fire cleanly.
ea_prompt.budget_envelope: Effective session cap: 60 min (+ active Budget Lift if granted). TTG: 2 h wall-clock. Trusted_scope merge eligibility: yes (Sprint 11 EA-2 BlarAI-side working set is 2-3 new files, aggregate ~150-250 LOC; well under DEC-18 thresholds at 3000 LOC / 100 files). Devplatform-side is a single-file surgical amendment, ~10-20 net new lines.
ea_prompt.links: - Continuation: docs/P5_TASK11_SDO_CONTINUATION_v1.0.xml - Sprint 11 SDV (v3): docs/sprints/sprint_11/strategic_design_vision.md - DEC-13 report format: docs/DEC13_REPORT_QUEUE_PROPOSAL_v1.xml - LA SDV v2 parallel-EA amendment: Vikunja #410 comment 550 (2026-05-11) - Sprint 8 SWAGR gap #5, Sprint 9 SWAGR gap #4, Sprint 10 SWAGR §15.3: Active State drift recurrence -- the 3-sprint motivation chain
